preFix.title: Wet-felted e-reader cover
preFix.subtitle: A close-fitting slip cover in dense merino felt, sized for a standard 6-inch e-reader
preFix.excerpt: A resist-felted slip cover for a standard 6-inch e-reader. Two layers of merino roving are felted over a card resist cut to device dimensions plus a 15% pre-shrink allowance. The finished cover is dense enough to protect the screen and holds its shape without padding.
preFix.sourceNotes: Resist-felted device covers are a well-established project format in UK wet-felting workshops. Technique synthesised from general resist felting practice; not drawn from a specific PD source.

content.text: This cover is resist-felted to the dimensions of a standard 6-inch e-reader (approximately 16 × 11.5 × 0.9 cm). A card
resist
is cut to the device dimensions plus 15% on each side to allow for shrinkage.
Roving
is wrapped around the resist in two layers, wetted, and rubbed to
pre-felt
stage before the resist is removed. The cover is then
fulled

until it is dense enough to protect the e-reader screen from light impact and scratches. The finished interior is smooth and non-scratching. Two layers of merino roving are used rather than four, because a thinner felt gives a snugger fit; adding padding would make the opening too tight.

items.name: Card resist: 18.5 × 13.5 cm (dimensions of a 6-inch e-reader plus 15% pre-shrink allowance)

content.text: Method
Step 1: Prepare the resist

Measure your e-reader: for a standard 6-inch model, it is approximately 16 × 11.5 cm. Add 15% to each dimension: 16 × 1.15 = 18.4 cm; 11.5 × 1.15 = 13.2 cm. Cut the card resist to these dimensions (approximately 18.5 × 13.5 cm). If your device is different, calculate 15% larger than the actual device size. Round all corners by about 1 cm so the cover will have rounded corners too.
Step 2: Lay the first roving layers on one face

Place the resist on the felting mat. Pull thin wisps of roving and lay them horizontally across the face of the card, extending 3 cm beyond each edge. Add a second layer running vertically, again extending 3 cm beyond each edge. Two layers on this face only. Keep the roving wisps thin and even.
Step 3: Wet and begin to felt the first face

Drizzle warm soapy water over the roving until fully saturated. Rub with flat hands in small circles for 8 minutes until the roving begins to hold together. The fibres should form a skin on the surface but the piece should still feel flexible.
Step 4: Fold the edges and cover the second face

Flip the resist over carefully. Fold the overhanging 3 cm border of roving from the first face neatly over the edges of the card onto the second face. This creates the walls of the pouch. Lay two fresh layers of roving on the second face to cover it, including the folded-over border. Wet and rub as before for 8 minutes.
Step 5: Roll in the bamboo mat

Roll the bamboo mat tightly around the piece and roll back and forth on the table for 10 minutes. Unroll, turn 90 degrees, and roll for another 10 minutes. Check that the roving is bonding on both faces and is not peeling away from the edges of the resist.
Step 6: Remove the resist and cut the opening

When the pre-felt is firm enough to handle without losing its shape, slide the card resist out carefully. Use scissors to cut a straight opening across one of the short ends, approximately 1 cm in from the edge of the felt. This opening is where the e-reader is inserted.
Step 7: Full to a snug fit

Return the open cover to warm soapy water. Scrunch, squeeze, and rub the entire surface. Check the fit by trying to slip your actual e-reader inside every 5 minutes: the cover is done when the device fits snugly with a slight resistance when pushed in, but not so tight that it cannot be inserted without force. The interior must feel smooth. If the felt is rough inside, turn the cover inside out and rub the interior briefly.
Step 8: Rinse and dry on the device

Rinse in water at the same temperature as the wash water (30-40°C). Insert the e-reader (wrapped in a plastic bag to protect it from moisture) and leave it in place while the cover dries. This shapes the cover exactly to the device dimensions. Remove the e-reader once the cover is fully dry.

items.fix: Return the cover to hot soapy water and full more aggressively: scrunch, throw, and rub for another 15 minutes. Check fit every 5 minutes.
items.cause: Not enough fulling was done after the resist was removed, or the resist was cut too generously
items.problem: The cover is too loose: the e-reader slides out easily

items.fix: While the cover is wet and warm, use your fingers to stretch the opening and the body gently. Insert a slightly oversize object (a hardback book wrapped in plastic) while drying.
items.cause: Fulling was continued past the point where the cover fit the device
items.problem: The cover is too tight: the e-reader cannot be inserted

items.fix: Ensure the border fold from the first face is smooth and even before adding the second face layers. In future, weigh the roving and divide equally between the two faces.
items.cause: Uneven roving distribution between the two faces
items.problem: One side of the cover is thicker than the other

content.text: Care and finishing

Hand-wash in cool water only; hot water will shrink the cover further. Press flat and dry with the e-reader (in a plastic bag) inside. The cover can be decorated on the outside with needle-felted motifs once dry, but keep embellishments flat so the cover slides easily in and out of bags.
